step_by_step_thinking:
I agree that option C, "Nuchal rigidity," should be excluded as it often indicates meningitis rather than pure non-paralytic polio. However, let me add another point of distinction; head drop (option B), where patients involuntarily lower their heads forward against resistance, is more commonly observed in spinal cord involvement, characteristic of paralytic polio rather than isolated non-paralytic cases. Thus, focusing specifically on nuchal rigidity strengthens our case further by highlighting an uncommon finding in genuine non-paralytic instances.
